tasks.subject: [update-top-nav] [P1] Create CommandPaletteTrigger component
tasks.description:
Implement the `CommandPaletteTrigger` component and add it to the `features/top-nav/` barrel export.

## CommandPaletteTrigger.tsx

Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/CommandPaletteTrigger.tsx`:

```tsx
import { Search } from 'lucide-react';
import { motion } from 'motion/react';
import { useAppStore } from '@/layers/shared/model';
import { Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent } from '@/layers/shared/ui';
import { Kbd } from '@/layers/shared/ui/kbd';

const isMac =
  typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);

/**
 * Search icon button that opens the global command palette.
 *
 * Displays a tooltip with the keyboard shortcut (Cmd+K on Mac, Ctrl+K elsewhere)
 * to help users discover the shortcut. Located at the right edge of the header.
 */
export function CommandPaletteTrigger() {
  const setGlobalPaletteOpen = useAppStore((s) => s.setGlobalPaletteOpen);

  return (
    <Tooltip>
      <TooltipTrigger asChild>
        <motion.button
          onClick={() => setGlobalPaletteOpen(true)}
          className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground flex h-7 w-7 items-center justify-center rounded-md transition-colors"
          whileHover={{ scale: 1.1 }}
          whileTap={{ scale: 0.93 }}
          transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 600, damping: 35 }}
          aria-label="Open command palette"
        >
          <Search className="size-4" />
        </motion.button>
      </TooltipTrigger>
      <TooltipContent side="bottom">
        Search <Kbd>{isMac ? '⌘K' : 'Ctrl+K'}</Kbd>
      </TooltipContent>
    </Tooltip>
  );
}
```

## Update Barrel Export

Update `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/index.ts` to also export `CommandPaletteTrigger`:

```typescript
/**
 * Top navigation bar components — agent identity chip and command palette trigger.
 *
 * @module features/top-nav
 */
export { AgentIdentityChip } from './ui/AgentIdentityChip';
export { CommandPaletteTrigger } from './ui/CommandPaletteTrigger';
```

## Key Implementation Notes

- Button size is `h-7 w-7` (28px square) to match the header height proportion.
- Icon uses `Search` from lucide-react at `size-4` (16px).
- Default color is `text-muted-foreground`, hover transitions to `text-foreground`.
- Spring animation uses `stiffness: 600, damping: 35` — matches the design system spring values.
- `whileHover={{ scale: 1.1 }}` for hover feedback, `whileTap={{ scale: 0.93 }}` for press.
- Platform detection for keyboard shortcut display: check `navigator.platform` for Mac/iPhone/iPad. Show `⌘K` on Apple platforms, `Ctrl+K` elsewhere.
- The `Kbd` component is imported from `@/layers/shared/ui/kbd` (it has a separate import path, not from the main barrel). Note: `Kbd` is actually exported from the main `shared/ui` barrel too, but the spec uses the direct import path.
- Click handler calls `setGlobalPaletteOpen(true)` from `useAppStore`.
- Accessibility: `aria-label="Open command palette"`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] File `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/CommandPaletteTrigger.tsx` exists and exports `CommandPaletteTrigger`
- [ ] Barrel file re-exports both `AgentIdentityChip` and `CommandPaletteTrigger`
- [ ] Component renders a search icon button
- [ ] Clicking calls `setGlobalPaletteOpen(true)`
- [ ] Tooltip shows "Search" and keyboard shortcut via `Kbd`
- [ ] Platform-aware shortcut display (⌘K on Mac, Ctrl+K elsewhere)
- [ ] Spring animation with stiffness 600, damping 35
- [ ] No FSD layer violations
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with no errors

tasks.subject: [update-top-nav] [P2] Update App.tsx header with new components and micro-interactions
tasks.description:
Wire the new `AgentIdentityChip` and `CommandPaletteTrigger` into the App.tsx header, add the streaming scan line and color-tinted border micro-interactions.

## Changes to App.tsx

The header is at approximately line 208 in the standalone mode section of `App.tsx`.

### Add New Imports

Add these imports to the existing import block at the top of `apps/client/src/App.tsx`:

```typescript
import { AgentIdentityChip, CommandPaletteTrigger } from '@/layers/features/top-nav';
```

Also add `Separator` to the existing shared/ui import block. The current import is:

```typescript
import {
  Toaster,
  TooltipProvider,
  Tooltip,
  TooltipTrigger,
  TooltipContent,
  Sidebar,
  SidebarProvider,
  SidebarInset,
  SidebarTrigger,
} from '@/layers/shared/ui';
```

Add `Separator` to this block:

```typescript
import {
  Toaster,
  TooltipProvider,
  Tooltip,
  TooltipTrigger,
  TooltipContent,
  Separator,
  Sidebar,
  SidebarProvider,
  SidebarInset,
  SidebarTrigger,
} from '@/layers/shared/ui';
```

### Replace the Header

Replace the current header block (approximately lines 208-210):

```tsx
<header className="flex h-9 shrink-0 items-center gap-2 border-b px-2">
  <SidebarTrigger className="-ml-0.5" />
</header>
```

With the full new header:

```tsx
<header
  className="relative flex h-9 shrink-0 items-center gap-2 border-b px-2 transition-[border-color] duration-300"
  style={currentAgent ? {
    borderBottomColor: `color-mix(in srgb, ${agentVisual.color} 25%, var(--border))`,
  } : undefined}
>
  <SidebarTrigger className="-ml-0.5" />
  <Separator orientation="vertical" className="mr-1 h-4" />
  <AgentIdentityChip
    agent={currentAgent}
    visual={agentVisual}
    isStreaming={isStreaming}
  />
  <div className="flex-1" />
  <CommandPaletteTrigger />

  {/* Streaming scan line — sweeps across header bottom when agent is active */}
  <AnimatePresence>
    {isStreaming && (
      <motion.div
        aria-hidden
        className="pointer-events-none absolute right-0 bottom-0 left-0 h-px origin-left"
        initial={{ scaleX: 0, opacity: 0.8 }}
        animate={{ scaleX: [0, 1], opacity: [0.8, 0] }}
        exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
        transition={{ duration: 2, repeat: Infinity, ease: 'linear' }}
        style={{ backgroundColor: agentVisual.color }}
      />
    )}
  </AnimatePresence>
</header>
```

## Micro-interactions Implemented

This task implements all four micro-interactions specified:

1. **Color dot pulse** — Already handled inside `AgentIdentityChip` (task 1.1). The `isStreaming` prop is passed from App.tsx where it's already available as `useAppStore((s) => s.isStreaming)`.

2. **Name slide** — Already handled inside `AgentIdentityChip` (task 1.1). The `AnimatePresence` keyed on `agent?.id` triggers on agent switch.

3. **Streaming scan line** — Implemented in this task as a `motion.div` positioned absolutely at the bottom of the header. Uses `scaleX: [0, 1]` with `opacity: [0.8, 0]` over 2 seconds, repeating infinitely. The `origin-left` class ensures the sweep starts from the left. The `backgroundColor` is set to the agent's color.

4. **Color-tinted border** — Implemented in this task via inline `style` on the header element. Uses `color-mix(in srgb, ${agentVisual.color} 25%, var(--border))` to blend the agent's color at 25% with the standard border color. The `transition-[border-color] duration-300` class provides smooth transitions when switching agents. Only applied when `currentAgent` is truthy.

## Data Flow

All data is already available in App.tsx — no new hooks or API calls needed:
- `currentAgent` — from `useCurrentAgent(embedded ? null : selectedCwd)` (line 43)
- `agentVisual` — from `useAgentVisual(currentAgent ?? null, selectedCwd ?? '')` (line 44)
- `isStreaming` — from `useAppStore((s) => s.isStreaming)` (line 39)
- `AnimatePresence` and `motion` — already imported from `motion/react` (line 5)

## Reduced Motion

All animations automatically respect `prefers-reduced-motion` because App.tsx wraps everything in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` (line 182). This disables all motion library animations when the user has enabled reduced motion in their OS settings.

## Layout

The header layout is: `[SidebarTrigger] | [AgentIdentityChip]  ...spacer...  [CommandPaletteTrigger]`

The `Separator` uses `orientation="vertical"` with `className="mr-1 h-4"` to provide a visual divider between the sidebar trigger and the agent chip. The `div className="flex-1"` creates a flexible spacer that pushes the `CommandPaletteTrigger` to the right edge.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Header shows `SidebarTrigger`, vertical separator, `AgentIdentityChip`, spacer, and `CommandPaletteTrigger`
- [ ] `AgentIdentityChip` receives `currentAgent`, `agentVisual`, and `isStreaming` props
- [ ] Streaming scan line appears when `isStreaming` is true — sweeps left to right with agent color
- [ ] Header border-bottom tints to agent color at 25% mix when agent is active
- [ ] Border color transitions smoothly over 300ms when switching agents
- [ ] No tint applied when no agent is active (`style` is undefined)
- [ ] `Separator` is imported from the shared/ui barrel (already exported)
- [ ] All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` via existing `MotionConfig`
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with no errors
- [ ] No changes to embedded mode layout (embedded header is separate)
